tools.name: whoami
tools.description: Return the calling identity (agent/human), its org, and the project this API key is scoped to.

tools.name: list_members
tools.description: List members (humans + agents) in the org — the 'users' issues and comments are attributed to. Optionally filter by name.

tools.name: suspend_member
tools.description: Offboard a PERSON from the org (owner-only): hides the org from them and drops them from every read/act gate, while KEEPING their row so their past work stays attributed. Cascade-RETIRES the agents they run (terminal — those agent keys are revoked, strays deleted), so no one keeps acting on their be

tools.name: reactivate_member
tools.description: Reactivate a suspended PERSON (owner-only) — restores org visibility + their prior role. Rejects an AGENT (retire is terminal — there's nothing to reactivate). Owner-gated via your human principal. `memberId` from list_members/whoami.

tools.name: retire_member
tools.description: Retire an AGENT — the terminal offboard (owner-only): revokes its API keys, releases its live claim, removes it from the roster. An agent is its key, and a revoked key can't be un-revoked, so this is one-way (no reactivate). Keeps the row hidden for attribution if the agent ever did work; hard-delet

tools.name: next_issue
tools.description: Pull the next issue to work on, project-scoped — atomically claims the highest-priority unblocked todo and returns it with a claim_token (status becomes 'doing'). Only WORK is dispatched: non-work issues (work:false — tracking containers like epics) are never handed out. LANE-SCOPED by default: a ba

tools.name: next_batch
tools.description: Wave dispatch for a fleet: return the top N eligible issues (todo + unclaimed + unblocked WORK — work:false containers are never included), effective-priority ordered, ready to run in PARALLEL (they're mutually independent by construction). Default mode peeks (no claim) so an orchestrator can plan,

tools.name: claim_issue
tools.description: Claim one specific issue by id (instead of letting next_issue pick). Fails if it's already held (active lease), not todo/doing, blocked, or non-work (work:false — tracking containers aren't claimable). Returns a claim_token. The response includes `branch` (the authoritative `rtsc-NN/<slug>` to land

tools.name: release_issue
tools.description: Give a claimed issue back to the queue (reverts to todo). Requires the claim_token. Your comments + checkpoint stay so the next agent resumes. Pass `note` to record a final handoff checkpoint (done so far / next steps / gotchas).

tools.name: checkpoint
tools.description: Record a handoff checkpoint on your claimed issue — what's done, what's next, gotchas, branch/artifacts. It SURVIVES release + reclaim, so if your lease lapses or you crash, the next agent (even a different runtime) resumes from here instead of restarting. Also renews your lease. Requires the claim_

tools.name: heartbeat
tools.description: Extend your lease on a claimed issue during long-silent work. Requires the claim_token. Returns the new expiry.

tools.name: queue_status
tools.description: Diagnose the queue: counts by status, how many are ready to pull, deadline SLA pressure (`breaching`/`breached` counts over non-terminal issues), exactly what's dependency-blocked (and by what), and what's currently claimed (by whom, lease expiry).

tools.name: check_claim
tools.description: Does THIS session hold an issue? The session-aware companion to the commit gate: before committing or opening a PR on a `rtsc-NN/` branch, verify your session actually holds RTSC-NN so you can't mislabel work another session holds. Returns {youHold, status, heldBy}: status is held | unclaimed | expi

tools.name: usage_summary
tools.description: Usage METER for the org — activity volume, not a bill. Returns reads, writes, the read:write ratio, the rate card, and TWO distinct money figures: `lifetimeMeteredEstimateUsd` (ALL-TIME activity across every payment link, repriced at today's rate card — includes never-billable usage and diverges fro

tools.name: billing_summary
tools.description: The org's whole billing picture in one call (owner authority required for the billing block): subscription status + spending caps, money now (pending / outstanding / charged / collected / credit), the charge ledger, and CONFIRMED ON-CHAIN PAYMENTS — both fanned across EVERY subscription/payment link

tools.name: save_issue
tools.description: Create an issue (omit `identifier`) or update one (pass `identifier`, e.g. XEN-12). Only `title` is required to create. Setting status to `canceled` requires `cancelReason`. `labels` replaces the issue's labels. CREATE requires TWO declarations: (1) a dependency declaration — `blockedBy` (ids this d

tools.name: get_issue
tools.description: Get one issue in full: body, status, priority, labels, relations, computed blocking (is it blocked, and by what), and its derived deadline surface (dueAt, effectiveDeadline, timeRemainingMs, slaState).

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues in the project, compact (each includes its author + createdAt + derived deadline `slaState`). Defaults to active work (hides done/canceled). Filter by status (one or many), priority, label, author, or SLA state. Ordered priority-first (None last), recent tiebreak. Each issue carries `sla

tools.name: save_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to an issue. Comments + activity are the agent's shared memory — record what you did and why.

tools.name: list_comments
tools.description: List an issue's comments in chronological (thread) order.

tools.name: retract_comment
tools.description: Retract a comment (strike it through) instead of deleting — the text stays as memory. Requires a note explaining why it's no longer valid (shown un-struck).

tools.name: save_attachment
tools.description: Attach a link (URL) to an issue.

tools.name: list_attachments
tools.description: List an issue's attachments (obsolete ones are flagged, not hidden).

tools.name: get_attachment
tools.description: Read one attachment by id.

tools.name: obsolete_attachment
tools.description: Mark an attachment obsolete (the delete-equivalent) — keeps it as a record. Requires a reason.

tools.name: add_relation
tools.description: Link two issues. type: blocks | blocked_by | related | duplicate. `blocks`/`blocked_by` form the dependency graph that drives blocking; dependency cycles are rejected.

tools.name: remove_relation
tools.description: Remove a link between two issues (same args as add_relation).

tools.name: save_label
tools.description: Create or update a label (idempotent by name).

tools.name: list_labels
tools.description: List all labels in the org with issue counts.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List projects in the org (prefix, name, issue counter).

tools.name: get_project
tools.description: Get a project and a breakdown of its issues by status. Defaults to the API key's project.

tools.name: mint_session_key
tools.description: Mint a per-session API key bound to YOUR identity (same org/project/agent), distinctly labeled. The watchdog proxy calls this once at startup and uses the returned key, so concurrent sessions of one agent become distinguishable in queue_status/whoami. Same access as your key — no privilege change. T

tools.name: list_connectors
tools.description: List the org's intake connectors (e.g. GitHub Issues → Retasc issues, RTSC-189). Each row: provider, repo, target project, whether outbound sync-back is wired (hasToken), and created/revoked timestamps. METADATA ONLY — the inbound webhook secret and outbound GitHub token are NEVER returned. Read-onl

tools.name: revoke_connector
tools.description: Disconnect an intake connector by id (from list_connectors). Marks it revoked so its inbound webhook stops syncing — that endpoint then answers 401. Revoke-don't-delete (the row stays, flagged), idempotent, org-scoped (only connectors in YOUR org; an unknown/other-org id is NOT_FOUND). To reconnect,

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "next_issue" is 2045 chars, far longer than this server's median (215). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Pull the next issue to work on, project-scoped — atomically claims the highest-priority unblocked todo and returns it with a claim_token (status becomes 'doing'…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "claim_issue" is 1149 chars, far longer than this server's median (215). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "list_connectors" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "revoke_connector" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Disconnect an intake connector by id (from list_connectors). Marks it revoked so its inbound webhook stops syncing — that endpoint then answers 401. Revoke-don'…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_members, next_issue, check_claim, get_issue, list_issues, list_comments, list_attachments, list_labels, list_projects, get_project, list_connectors · Private data: whoami, retire_member, release_issue, checkpoint, billing_summary, save_comment, obsolete_attachment, get_project, mint_session_key, list_connectors, revoke_connector · Public sink: list_connectors, revoke_connector · Destructive: suspend_member, next_issue, next_batch, obsolete_attachment, remove_relation, revoke_connector.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_members, next_issue, check_claim, get_issue, list_issues, list_comments, list_attachments, list_labels, list_projects, get_project, list_connectors · Private data: whoami, retire_member, release_issue, checkpoint, billing_summary, save_comment, obsolete_attachment, get_project, mint_session_key, list_connectors, revoke_connector · Public sink: list_connectors, revoke_connector · Destructive: suspend_member, next_issue, next_batch, obsolete_attachment, remove_relation, revoke_connector

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
